summary: Documentation for Alauda Database for MySQL — a high-availability MySQL solution built on MySQL Group Replication and Kubernetes.

docs/en/apis/index.mdx.description: Landing page for the Alauda Database for MySQL API Reference section, rendering an `<Overview />` component that auto-indexes the API documentation subtree (Kubernetes APIs and CRD references). Acts as the navigation entry point users hit when browsing API docs in the rendered site.

docs/en/apis/kubernetes_apis/index.mdx.description: Hub page for the Kubernetes APIs section, presenting an `<Overview />` listing of available Kubernetes-native API references. Funnels readers into the MySQL-MGR CRD reference subtree (`mgr/` directory) covering `Mysql`, `MySQLBackup`, `MySQLRestore`, `MysqlSchedule`, and `MysqlUser` resources.

docs/en/apis/kubernetes_apis/mgr/01-mysq.description: Renders the CRD reference for `mysqls.middleware.alauda.io` via the `<K8sCrd>` component, exposing the full spec/status schema of the `Mysql` custom resource. This is the primary CRD used to declaratively provision a MySQL Group Replication cluster, configuring members, router replicas, resources, storage, monitoring, parameter templates, and version/upgrade options.

docs/en/apis/kubernetes_apis/mgr/05-mgr-.description: Renders the CRD reference for `mysqlbackups.mysql.middleware.alauda.io` via the `<K8sCrd>` component, documenting the `MySQLBackup` resource used to trigger one-off full or incremental backups of an MGR cluster. Surfaces the `spec.cluster`, `spec.storage.s3` (bucket/endpoint/region/secret), and `spec.type` fields consumed by the operator to write data to an external S3 target.

docs/en/apis/kubernetes_apis/mgr/10-mgr-.description: Renders the CRD reference for `mysqlrestores.middleware.alauda.io` via the `<K8sCrd>` component, documenting the `MySQLRestore` resource. Describes the `spec.mgr.backup.name`, `spec.mgr.cluster.name` (target instance), and `spec.mgr.source.name` (source instance) fields used to restore data from an existing `MySQLBackup` into a chosen MGR cluster.

docs/en/apis/kubernetes_apis/mgr/15-mgr-.description: Renders the CRD reference for `mysqlschedules.mysql.middleware.alauda.io` via the `<K8sCrd>` component, surfacing the `MysqlSchedule` resource used to configure automatic backup schedules. Documents fields such as `spec.expiryDays`, `spec.full.cron`, `spec.incr.cron`, and `spec.storage.s3` that drive periodic full and incremental backups to an S3 bucket with a configured retention window.

docs/en/apis/kubernetes_apis/mgr/20-mgr-.description: Renders the CRD reference for `mysqlusers.middleware.alauda.io` via the `<K8sCrd>` component, exposing the `MysqlUser` resource schema used to declaratively manage MySQL accounts. Surfaces `spec.user`, `spec.host`, `spec.mysql` (target instance), `spec.secretName`, and the `spec.privileges` list (grants on database/table targets) that the operator applies via SQL.

docs/en/apis/kubernetes_apis/mgr/index.m.description: Index page for the MySQL-MGR Kubernetes APIs subtree, presenting an `<Overview />` of the five CRD reference pages: `Mysql` (MGR clusters), `MySQLBackup`, `MySQLRestore`, `MysqlSchedule`, and `MysqlUser`. Serves as the navigation anchor when readers drill from the Kubernetes APIs section into MGR-specific resources.

docs/en/architecture.mdx.description: Explains the layered design of MySQL Group Replication (Group Replication Logic, Group Communication System, Paxos) and how Alauda deploys MGR on Kubernetes. Covers the deployment topology: MySQL members run as a StatefulSet with PVC-backed storage, MySQL Router runs as a Deployment fronted by two Services (`*-read-write` and `*-read-only`), and client traffic is routed through Router with writes synchronized to all nodes via Group Replication.

docs/en/functions/01-create.mdx.description: Step-by-step procedure for provisioning a MySQL-MGR instance, with parallel CLI and Web Console tabs. The CLI flow creates the per-instance password secret (`clusterchecker`, `exporter`, `manage`, `root` keys), applies a `middleware.alauda.io/v1 Mysql` manifest configuring `spec.mgr.members`, monitoring, server/router resources, `svcRO`/`svcRW`, `volumeClaimTemplate` (TopoLVM recommended), and a large `spec.params.mysql.mysqld` block (binlog, InnoDB, group replication tunables, thread pool). The console flow walks through single-master vs multi-master, replica count, resources, storage class, root password, and access mode.

docs/en/functions/05-delete.mdx.description: Procedure for removing a MySQL-MGR instance via `kubectl delete mysql` or the Web Console's **Actions > Delete** path. Calls out that the operator auto-removes safely-deletable child resources (StatefulSets, Deployments, ConfigMaps, Secrets, Services) but leaves PVCs and `mysqlbackups.mysql.middleware.alauda.io` resources for manual cleanup; provides the label-selector `kubectl get pvc -l v1alpha1.mysql.middleware.alauda.io/cluster=${name}` command to bulk-delete persistent volumes.

docs/en/functions/10-access.mdx.description: How to connect to a MySQL-MGR instance from inside or outside the cluster, covering both ClusterIP and NodePort exposure modes. Documents the auto-generated `${name}-read-write` and `${name}-read-only` Services, the `kubectl get svc ... -o jsonpath='{.spec.ports[0].nodePort}'` lookup for NodePort, the `mysql -h... -P... -u... -p...` connection command, and the `SELECT * FROM performance_schema.replication_group_members` query for inspecting MGR member roles.

docs/en/functions/15-user.mdx.description: User lifecycle workflow for MySQL-MGR clusters: create a per-user password secret (`host`/`user`/`password` keys), then apply a `MysqlUser` CR (`middleware.alauda.io/v1`) referencing the target `spec.mysql` instance, `spec.secretName`, and a `spec.privileges` list of grants (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, DROP, INDEX, ALTER, EXECUTE, EVENT, TRIGGER, ...) scoped to `${database}.*` targets. Includes Web Console steps and a `SHOW GRANTS` verification example.

docs/en/functions/20-backup_restore.mdx.description: End-to-end backup and restore workflow for MGR clusters, requiring an external S3 bucket and AWS-style secret. Covers configuring automatic backups by patching `mysqlschedules` with `expiryDays`, `full.cron`, `incr.cron`, and `storage.s3` fields; creating ad-hoc backups with a `mysql.middleware.alauda.io/v1 MySQLBackup` CR (`type: full` or `increment`); restoring with a `MySQLRestore` CR that references the source backup and target instance; and deleting backups (which also removes the underlying S3 data).

docs/en/functions/30-stop.mdx.description: Procedure for pausing and resuming an MGR instance to release compute while retaining configuration and PVC data. The CLI path patches `spec.pause` to `true`/`false` on the `mysql` resource (`kubectl patch mysql ... --type='merge' -p '{"spec":{"pause":true}}'`); the Web Console exposes the same via **Actions > Stop**/**Start**. Notes that stopping terminates all connections, recommends a full backup beforehand, and requires post-restart verification of MGR topology.

docs/en/functions/35-restart.mdx.description: Steps for restarting a MySQL-MGR instance, either by adding a `restartTime` annotation via `kubectl annotate mysql ${name}` or by clicking Actions > Restart in the Web Console under the MySQL-MGR namespace and instance view.

docs/en/functions/40-parameter.mdx.description: Parameter configuration reference for MySQL-MGR instances, covering how to apply built-in templates such as `ssd-2c4g-mysql-8.0-mgr`, override parameters via `spec.params.mysql.mysqld` in the Mysql CR (e.g. `max_connections`, `binlog_expire_logs_seconds`), and import or export bulk settings from the Parameter Configuration tab. Includes an extensive reference table of every supported MySQL 8.0 / Percona Server variable with defaults, application strategy (RestartApply, DynamicApply), and descriptions — spanning performance_schema instrumentation, InnoDB tuning, replication, binlog, SSL, audit log, and Group Replication parameters.

docs/en/functions/43-update-specificatio.description: Procedure for online expansion of MySQL-MGR resources without downtime: patch `spec.mgr.resources` with `kubectl patch mysql --type=merge` to change CPU/memory limits and requests, expand storage by patching `spec/mgr/volumeClaimTemplate/spec/resources/requests/storage` with a JSON patch, and verify PVC resize via `kubectl get pvc -l v1alpha1.mysql.middleware.alauda.io/cluster=`. Notes the 9-member maximum for Group Replication and the recommended minimum of 3 members for HA.

docs/en/functions/45-qan.mdx.description: Query analysis (QAN) workflow for identifying SQL statements that affect MySQL performance — from the instance's Query Analysis tab in the Web Console, set a Time Range filter or search by SQL statement keyword to surface slow or problematic queries for tuning.

docs/en/functions/50-logs.mdx.description: How to retrieve operational logs from a MySQL-MGR instance — list all mysql and mysql-router pods via `kubectl get pod -l middleware.instance/name=`, then stream container logs with `kubectl logs`, or use the Web Console's Realtime Log tab to view MySQL error logs for fault localization.

docs/en/functions/55-monitor.mdx.description: Reference for the built-in MySQL-MGR monitoring panel covering pod-level metrics (CPU, memory, network, PVC usage) and MySQL-specific indicators including Max Connections, Threads Connected/Running, table locks, sorts, slow queries, InnoDB buffer pool requests, TPS, and command counts. Real-time data appears under the instance's Monitoring tab and feeds notification strategies for alerting.

docs/en/functions/60-scheduling.mdx.description: Procedure for updating a MySQL-MGR instance's scheduling configuration (Pod tolerations) via Actions > Update Scheduling Configuration in the Web Console, with constraints that Pod migration is not supported and the set of nodes matching new tolerations must include the previously available nodes plus any additions.

docs/en/functions/index.mdx.description: Section landing page that uses the `<Overview />` component to enumerate the Functions guides — instance creation, monitoring, parameter and specification updates, restarts, scheduling, query analysis, and logs.

docs/en/how_to/30-upgrade.mdx.description: Walkthrough for Patch Version upgrades of MySQL-MGR — set a Manual or Automatic Patch Version Upgrade Strategy on the instance detail page, trigger per-instance upgrades from the in-page upgrade prompt, or perform Bulk Patch Version Upgrade by selecting target instances under a specific operator in Upgrade Management (requires platform administrator permissions). Patch upgrades run rolling updates.

docs/en/how_to/35-migrate-from-pxc.mdx.description: End-to-end migration guide from Percona XtraDB Cluster (PXC) 5.7 to MySQL Group Replication (MGR) 8.0 on Alauda Container Platform v4.0+, covering schema compatibility checks for reserved keywords/ZEROFILL/invalid date defaults, utf8mb4 character set conversion, target Mysql CR creation, streaming `mysqldump --single-transaction --set-gtid-purged=ON --routines --events --triggers` into the MGR cluster, user/grant migration via `SHOW CREATE USER`+`SHOW GRANTS`, verification across 9 object categories, application cutover to `<name>-read-write.<ns>.svc.cluster.local:3306`, post-migration `ANALYZE TABLE`/`OPTIMIZE TABLE`, rollback procedures, and fixes for GTID_PURGED, DEFINER, and `caching_sha2_password` authentication errors.

docs/en/how_to/40-deploy-without-topolvm.description: Deployment guide for MySQL-MGR on clusters without TopoLVM (e.g. MicroOS with read-only root, bare-metal, evaluation environments), using a `kubernetes.io/no-provisioner` StorageClass with `WaitForFirstConsumer` binding, manually provisioned local PVs under `/opt/local-pv/pvN` with `chmod 777` and per-node `kubernetes.io/hostname` nodeAffinity, and the Mysql CR set to `storageClassName: mgr-local-pv`. Also covers configuring `RdsInstaller.spec.slowSQLCK.hostPath` before installing query-analytics-operator to avoid the `mkdir /cpaas: read-only file system` ClickHouse failure, plus recovery options (recreating `qai` or editing it directly) and troubleshooting Pending PVCs, CrashLoopBackOff, Released PVs, and ErrorReconcile states.

docs/en/how_to/50-access.mdx.description: Connection patterns for accessing MySQL-MGR instances from applications — in-cluster access via the read-write/read-only SVCs (e.g. `mgr-read-write:32631`, `mgr-read-only:32630`) and external access via NodePort across multiple node IPs. Includes JDBC examples using multi-host `jdbc:mysql://mgr-0:3306,mgr-1:3306,mgr-2:3306` URLs and GORM examples using the `dbresolver` plugin with separate Sources/Replicas and `RandomPolicy` for both single- and multi-master modes.

docs/en/how_to/index.mdx.description: Section landing page rendering the HowTo guides index via the `<Overview />` component — links to operational guides such as Patch Version upgrades, migration from MySQL-PXC, deploying without TopoLVM, and accessing MGR instances from applications.

docs/en/index.mdx.description: Top-level navigation page for the Alauda Database for MySQL (MySQL-MGR) documentation site, rendered through the `<Overview overviewHeaders={[]} />` component to expose the introduction, installation, concepts, install guide, hands-on, functions, hands-on, APIs, and permissions sections.

docs/en/installation.mdx.description: Installation walkthrough for the three plugins required to use Alauda Database for MySQL — Alauda Container Platform Data Services Essentials (optional, foundational for the Data Services Web Console, installed to the `global` cluster via Marketplace > Cluster Plugins), Alauda Container Platform Data Services RDS Framework (installed from OperatorHub on any cluster needing Data Services views), and the Alauda Database Service for MySQL operator itself (installed from OperatorHub, default `alpha` channel, Cluster install mode). Each section lists prerequisites and the recommended OperatorHub install parameters.

docs/en/intro.mdx.description: Introduces Alauda Database for MySQL as a Kubernetes-native high-availability solution built on MySQL Group Replication, summarizing key capabilities (operator-driven lifecycle management, automatic failover, elastic node addition/removal, and MySQL Router-based read-write separation). Enumerates concrete usage constraints: a 9-member replication group ceiling (3+ recommended), InnoDB-only with `disabled_storage_engines` guidance, mandatory explicit/invisible primary keys (`sql_generate_invisible_primary_key`), low-latency network requirement, READ COMMITTED isolation (no SERIALIZABLE), no cascading foreign keys or concurrent DDL/DML in multi-master mode, `group_replication_transaction_size_limit` (~143 MB) and `group_replication_member_expel_timeout` (5s) bounds, no heterogeneous architectures (MySQL Clone plugin limitation), and rolling-restart semantics that flip secondaries before the primary.

docs/en/release_notes.mdx.description: Tracks Alauda Database Services for MySQL-MGR releases v4.0.0 and v4.0.1, documenting fixed-issue and known-issue lists via embedded Middleware project templates (`mw-mgr-v4.0.1-fixed`, `mw-mgr-v4.0-fixed`, `mw-mgr-v4.0-known`). The v4.0.0 entry highlights two product changes: component-platform decoupling (MySQL-MGR lifecycle is now independent of the platform release, enabling on-demand feature/bug shipments) and real-time log streaming on the component details page for faster troubleshooting.

docs/en/upgrade.mdx.description: Operator upgrade runbook centered on the v4.3.0 removal of MySQL-PXC: explains Automatic vs. Manual upgrade strategies and a mandatory pre-upgrade checklist for clusters with `perconaxtradbcluster` resources, covering identification (`kubectl get perconaxtradbcluster -A`), pre-migration compatibility checks (MySQL 8.0 reserved keywords, ZEROFILL/invalid-date defaults, non-`utf8mb4` tables, `gtid_mode`/`enforce_gtid_consistency`), target MGR 8.0 instance sizing (2-3x storage), `mysqldump` full-backup migration, verification, and PXC CR/PVC decommission. Details four upgrade scenarios (no PXC, migrate-first, upgrade-first-migrate-later, mixed clusters) plus an emergency response plan for stuck PXC pods, suspected data inconsistency, and failed migration windows, with the explicit caveat that the MySQL operator cannot be rolled back after upgrade.
